title: GitHub - bruno-de-queiroz/hoop: Being alone is not a requirement. A sandboxed and web harness on top of claude code.
excerpt: Being alone is not a requirement. A sandboxed and web harness on top of claude code. - bruno-de-queiroz/hoop
content_html:
hoop-showcase.mp4

Being alone is not a requirement. A sandboxed and web harness on top of Claude Code. Two deliverables in one install:

  1. /hoop:setup — an interactive wizard that wires a curated stack (memory, code-graph RAG, automation, platform MCPs, docs RAG, observability, design, second-brain).
  2. /hoop:dashboard — a containerized local web dashboard (Next.js, http://localhost:7842/) with live sessions, a skill browser with one-click triggers, a nested sub-agent tree, push-based event observability, and BM25 + optional semantic search across all events.

The plugin does not re-implement any of the third-party MCPs or skills it installs. It picks, installs, documents, and observes them.

Install

There are two ways in. Pick the one that matches your host.

A · Inside Claude Code (you already run Claude Code)

/plugin marketplace add bruno-de-queiroz/hoop
/plugin install hoop@hoop-marketplace
/plugin list
/reload-plugins
/hoop:setup

(The /plugin list + /reload-plugins dance is required on Claude Code v2.1.138 to activate a freshly pre-seeded plugin in the current session. New sessions don't need it.)

B · Standalone — no Claude Code on the host

You do not need Claude Code (or even Node) installed on the host. The sandbox ships its own claude binary and authenticates with its own Claude account, so a bare machine with Docker is enough.

Prerequisites

  • Docker — Docker Desktop, or any Docker engine + Compose v2 (docker compose). This is the only hard dependency for hoop start — Claude Code, Node, gh, uv, gws, and the dashboard all live in containers, never on your host, and the sandbox seeds its own Claude profile on boot (so hoop start needs no host jq). A few other subcommands do shell out to small host tools — see the optional list below.
  • git and bash — to clone the repo and run the CLI.
  • A web browser you can reach — for the one-time login approval. It does not have to be on the same machine; you just need to open a URL and copy a code back (see step 4). Handy for headless servers.
  • A Claude account with an active plan (Pro / Max / Team / Enterprise) that can sign in via /login.
  • jq (needed by some subcommands, not hoop start) — required by hoop setup and hoop logout, which edit JSON on the host (the wizard reads your gh/Claude identity into profile.md; logout rewrites the sandbox credentials). hoop open uses it too but degrades with a warning if it's absent. Install: macOS brew install jq; Debian/Ubuntu sudo apt-get install -y jq.
  • curl (optional — never strictly required) — used by hoop setup to probe Docker Model Runner and by the dashboard health-wait; both fall back to a bash /dev/tcp probe when it's missing. Install: brew install curl / sudo apt-get install -y curl.
  • awk (needed only for hoop mount) — required by hoop mount add/remove to rewrite the mounts list; unused by every other command.
  • Docker Model Runner (optional) — for semantic-search embeddings. hoop setup enables it (Docker Desktop AI, or the docker-model-plugin on Docker Engine) and appends the embedding model to the compose stack via Compose's models: element (Docker Compose v2.38+), so hoop start auto-pulls it and wires the endpoint into the sandbox. If DMR is ever unreachable — or Compose is older than v2.38 — hoop start transparently drops the override and falls back to BM25 rather than failing. Skippable — Ollama, OpenAI, or a custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint also work.

Run hoop doctor at any time to verify the host has everything and see how close you are to a clean Docker-only setup.

Steps

# 1. Clone and enter the repo
git clone https://github.com/bruno-de-queiroz/hoop && cd hoop

# 2. (optional) Put `hoop` on your PATH + shell completion.
#    Skip this and prefix every command with ./plugins/hoop/cli/hoop.sh instead.
./plugins/hoop/cli/hoop.sh install      # then open a new shell so `hoop` resolves

# 3. Bring up the whole stack. The FIRST run builds both images (a few minutes);
#    later starts are fast. Serves the dashboard at http://localhost:7842/.
hoop start

# 4. (optional) Configure your stack interactively — memory, code-graph RAG,
#    platform MCPs, docs RAG, semantic search, observability, second-brain, etc.
#    This is the /hoop:setup wizard, native to the CLI (needs no host Claude Code).
hoop setup

# 5. One-time: authenticate the sandbox with its OWN Claude account.
#    Drops you into `claude` inside the sandbox — type /login, open the printed
#    URL in your browser, approve, paste the code back, then /exit.
hoop login

Then open http://localhost:7842/. Because the dashboard only trusts localhost, your browser is recognized as the host automatically — the access token is minted for you, nothing to paste. (Remote access is deliberately gated; see Architecture.)

That's the full loop: hoop start → hoop setup (optional) → hoop login → open the dashboard. A few notes for the standalone path:

  • Configure your stack with hoop setup. This is the interactive wizard (memory, code-graph RAG, automation, platform MCPs, docs RAG, semantic search, observability, design, second-brain, telemetry isolation) — the same one behind /hoop:setup, but native to the CLI so it needs no host Claude Code. It runs before hoop login (writing MCP config needs no auth). For one-off additions later, hoop add mcp/plugin/skill (see the CLI table) persists into the sandbox profile across rebuilds.
  • Terminal-only? hoop open runs an interactive claude in a throwaway sandbox over your current directory — no dashboard, no browser needed after login.
  • Updating: git pull then hoop rebuild picks up new code into both images.
  • Signing out / switching accounts: hoop logout clears the sandbox's Claude login so a different account can hoop login.

What the wizard does

hoop setup (or /hoop:setup, which points you to it) walks you through these steps with one consent at the top, then installs each pick — auto-runnable and secret-taking MCPs run immediately (every command printed first); browser-login, plugin-marketplace, and host-CLI options are printed as guided steps to finish yourself:

# Step Pick
1 Consent Y / N
2 Detect prior state (read-only)
3 Memory claude-mem (installed automatically — no choice)
4 Code-graph RAG (if you code) Serena / claude-context / code-graph-mcp / Cognee / skip
5 Automation n8n-mcp yes / no
6 Platform MCPs multi-select: Atlassian, Google Workspace, GitHub, incident.io, Slack
7 Docs RAG Context7 yes / no
8 Observability Sentry / Datadog (multi-select)
9 Design Excalidraw yes / no
10 Second-brain Obsidian (3 flavors) / Notion (2) / Logseq / NotebookLM
11 Sign-ins Claude Code /login, then gh (device flow) + any queued MCP OAuth — all inside the sandbox

Each run appends to the sandbox profile's ~/.claude/hoop/sandbox/profile/.claude/hoop/install-log.md (also viewable from the dashboard) so re-runs are auditable. Secrets never reach the log — they go straight to claude mcp add -e or the 0600 ~/.claude/hoop/hoop.env.

Dashboard

/hoop:dashboard (or start | stop | restart | rebuild | status | logs) runs the dashboard inside a container. Your host only needs Docker Desktop — no Node, no npm install, no Next.js build pollution. Each verb takes an optional service target (all (default) · sandbox · dashboard); start builds lazily (only when an image is missing) while rebuild always rebuilds.

Pairing (inviting a teammate to co-drive a session) uses cloudflared to expose the local dashboard over a public tunnel — it's baked into the dashboard image, so nothing to install on the host. The dashboard runs fine without pairing; only share links start a tunnel.

Five panels:

  • Sessions — fs.watch on ~/.claude/sessions/; updates in real time.
  • Skills — every skill on disk (user + plugin), filterable, with a one-click "Run" that spawns claude -p '/<name>' inside the dashboard container and streams stdout back to the panel.
  • Sub-agents — nested tree reconstructed from PreToolUse / PostToolUse events on the Agent tool. Click a node to see its prompt, tool calls, and final output.
  • Events — chronological live tail via Server-Sent Events. Hooks push each event to the sandbox's /ingest over the Unix domain socket; the dashboard tails the resulting stream with zero polling.
  • Search — opens with ⌘K. BM25 (FTS5) always works; semantic search (sqlite-vec, 768-dim embeddings) activates when an embedding backend is configured. Set one up via hoop setup / /hoop:setup — recommended is Docker Model Runner (added to the compose stack via Compose's models: element and pulled on hoop start), with Ollama (nomic-embed-text), OpenAI, or any custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint as alternatives. Hybrid fuses BM25 + semantic via Reciprocal Rank Fusion.

The dashboard is single-user and localhost-only by design; access is gated by a per-install token (see Architecture below).

CLI (hoop)

plugins/hoop/cli/ ships a small oosh-based CLI that wraps the runtime. It lives inside the plugin (framework engine + entry point + completions + the stack engine in lib/stack.sh) so it ships with the plugin and the slash commands (/hoop:setup, /hoop:dashboard) can invoke it directly. It needs no external install and resolves its own paths.

./plugins/hoop/cli/hoop.sh install     # symlink `hoop` onto PATH + shell completion (bash/zsh)
# or run in place without installing:
./plugins/hoop/cli/hoop.sh <module> <command>

Two levels: top-level verbs act on the whole stack; modules scope a single service. All of them drive one engine (cli/lib/stack.sh) — the single source of truth for host-side preflight (profile bind-mount prep, dashboard/peer auth tokens, forwarded embedding env, compose orchestration). The Claude onboarding bypass + plugin/hook wiring run inside the sandbox on boot (sandbox/seed-profile.mjs), so the host needs no jq. start/rebuild are deliberately split — start only builds an image when it's missing (fast otherwise), while rebuild always rebuilds so you pick up code changes.

Command What it does
hoop start · stop · restart · rebuild · status · logs Whole stack (agent-sandbox + dashboard) via the engine (up -d / down / build + up -d --force-recreate for the project). rebuild takes -n|--no-cache.
hoop login · logout Authenticate the sandbox with its own Claude account (one-time). login drops you into claude inside the running sandbox to run /login (paste-code flow — approve the URL in your host browser); the sandbox mints and self-refreshes its own OAuth token. logout clears it so you can sign in as a different account. Your host credentials are never read or copied.
Module Commands What it does
dashboard start · stop · restart · rebuild · status · logs Controls only the dashboard UI container (--no-deps, leaves agent-sandbox alone). rebuild takes -n|--no-cache.
sandbox start · stop · restart · rebuild · update Controls only the agent-sandbox container. Lifecycle verbs run the shared engine scoped to the sandbox (so plugin wiring + forwarded env still happen); rebuild recreates the container (-n|--no-cache to skip the layer cache); update pins the baked-in claude-code version (-c|--claude-version).
open (default) Runs a fresh, telemetry-isolated sandbox over the current directory: mounts $PWD read-write into the agent workspace and launches claude interactively (docker run --rm -it, real tty for the TUI). Forces HOOP_DISABLE_TELEMETRY=1 (add -T|--telemetry to allow bundled-tool telemetry) and strips the dashboard-only hooks + the hoop plugin from an overlay settings.json, while keeping credentials, setup MCPs, skills, and other plugins. Extra args pass straight through, e.g. hoop open --model opus or hoop open "fix the failing test".
add mcp · plugin · skill Installs a component into the sandbox profile so it persists across rebuild/restart/recreate and is shared by both dashboard sessions and hoop open (the profile is bind-mounted into both). mcp <name> [flags] [-- <cmd>] → claude mcp add, defaulting to --scope user so the server is global (not stranded under one project — pass your own -s|--scope to override); plugin [-m|--marketplace <spec>] <plugin[@marketplace]> → claude plugin install; skill -d|--dir <dir> [-f|--force] copies a local skill directory (must contain SKILL.md) into ~/.claude/skills/<name>, dereferencing symlinks so the real files resolve inside the container. mcp/plugin need the sandbox running; skill is host-only.
mount add · list · remove Bind-mounts host folders into the sandbox workspace at /home/agent/workspace/<name>. add -p|--path <host-dir> [-n|--name <name>] adds one (the -p path tab-completes directories), list shows the configured mounts, remove <name> drops one. Mounts persist via a generated compose override; each add/remove recreates the agent-sandbox container.
install (default) Bare hoop install symlinks hoop onto PATH + wires shell completion (-f|--force reinstalls over an existing symlink). The repo itself is never modified; hoop uninstall removes the symlink and shell wiring.
setup (default) Runs the interactive stack wizard (the native port of /hoop:setup): boots the sandbox, then walks memory / code-graph / automation / platform MCPs / docs RAG / semantic search / observability / design / second-brain / telemetry, installing each pick into the sandbox profile. Auto/secret MCPs run via claude mcp add; OAuth/plugin/host-CLI sign-ins complete at the end inside the sandbox. Needs a TTY (refuses head-less), runs before hoop login, writes profile.md + install-log.md. --reset-first wipes all sandbox state for a blank slate first.
doctor (default) Read-only health check of the host + stack through a Docker-only-standalone lens: verifies Docker + Compose v2, reports which optional subcommand tools (jq/curl/awk) are present, checks the sandbox image/containers, Claude auth, and the semantic-search backend (incl. the DMR Compose models: override + reachability). Fails only on truly blocking problems; everything else is advisory. 
hoop start                    # bring up the whole stack at http://localhost:7842/
hoop setup                    # interactive wizard: configure the sandbox stack (no host Claude Code needed)
hoop login                    # one-time: authenticate the sandbox with its own Claude account
hoop dashboard rebuild        # rebuild + recreate ONLY the dashboard container
hoop sandbox rebuild          # rebuild + recreate ONLY the agent-sandbox container
hoop open                     # interactive claude in a sandbox over $PWD
hoop add mcp context7 -- npx -y @upstash/context7-mcp   # install an MCP (user scope) into the sandbox
hoop add skill -d ~/.claude/skills/impeccable           # copy a local skill into the sandbox profile
hoop mount add -p ~/code/myproject                      # expose a host folder to the sandbox workspace

The add / mount subcommands are also exposed as the /hoop:add and /hoop:mount slash commands, which delegate to this CLI. Everything add writes lives in ~/.claude/hoop/sandbox/profile (bind-mounted at /home/agent), so a component installed once is available in every dashboard session and in hoop open, and survives image rebuilds.

hoop open uses the hoop-sandbox image (build it once with hoop sandbox rebuild) and mounts the sandbox Claude profile (~/.claude/hoop/sandbox/profile, -p\|--profile to override) so claude is already authenticated — run hoop login once to authenticate the sandbox with its own Claude account. Unlike the dashboard's agent-sandbox, open runs with telemetry blackholed and without hoop's dashboard hooks/plugin (which need the dashboard's socket), so it's a clean, isolated interactive session that still has your setup MCPs and skills. hoop install / hoop uninstall manage the PATH symlink and shell wiring; the repo itself is never modified.

Browser automation

The hoop-sandbox image ships a headless Chromium + the official @playwright/mcp, registered in the sandbox profile automatically (from inside the container, via claude mcp add, so the registration can't point at a browser the running image doesn't have). The agent gets browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_type, browser_fill_form, browser_snapshot, browser_take_screenshot, and more — driven by a browser that runs entirely in-container as the unprivileged agent user. No host browser, no host process, no host.docker.internal gymnastics, and nothing runs in your user context. Because the same image + profile back both surfaces, browser tools are available in every dashboard session and in hoop open.

Two deliberate hardening choices:

  • Ephemeral profile (--isolated). The browser profile is kept in memory and never written to disk, so no cookies or logins persist across sessions and there's no ambient auth lying around. To drive a site that needs a login, hand the browser tools a --storage-state file (see the @playwright/mcp docs).
  • No arbitrary-code tool. @playwright/mcp exposes browser_run_code_unsafe (arbitrary JavaScript in the Playwright process, RCE-equivalent) as a non-removable "core" capability. hoop denies it via Claude's own permissions.deny in the sandbox settings.json, so the model never sees it — the rest of the toolset is unaffected.

Pairing & plan review

/hoop:dashboard can hand a share link to a teammate over a cloudflared tunnel. They open it, pick a name, and the host admits them; from that point both sides see the same live transcript and can chat (> prefix) or co-drive the model — from a laptop or a phone.

Run a turn with /plan <task> and the sandbox forces the agent read-only: it investigates, then submits a plan that opens in a review panel. The host and any full-capability peer can drop inline comments anchored to the exact passage — synced live across everyone — then Approve or Request changes. A rejection feeds the comments back and the agent revises the plan.

The host's flow, end to end

1 · Chat — a peer joins and both sides chat in-thread (> prefix); no model turn is spent.
2 · Prompt — the host sends a normal turn to the model.

3 · Clarify — the agent asks structured questions before planning.
4 · Observe — every hook event streams into the live Events panel.

5 · Plan — /plan forces the agent read-only; it drafts a plan instead of acting.
6 · Review — the plan opens for inline comments, then Approve / Request changes.


7 · Iterate — “Request changes” feeds the comments back; the agent revises read-only and resubmits, and once the host approves the session leaves plan mode and implements it.

Peers co-drive the same session from anywhere — here's “Rafael” reviewing the plan from his phone:

Architecture

The runtime is split across two containers so a compromise of the web layer can't reach your credentials:

  • agent-sandbox (trusted) — owns the claude binary, your Claude profile (OAuth credentials, plugins, MCP config, sessions/transcripts), the long-lived claude -p --input-format=stream-json subprocesses, and events.db (sole writer). It exposes a small HTTP API over a Unix domain socket (/var/run/hoop/sandbox.sock) — no TCP port. The model runs as an unprivileged agent user, and the claude-facing plugin surface the sandbox actually uses (hook scripts + the .mcp.json tools server + the plugin manifest) is baked into the sandbox image at /opt/hoop, root-owned so that user can't tamper with the hook scripts. Host-only pieces are deliberately not baked: the commands/ (host-stack slash commands) and agents/ (host diagnostics) aren't meaningful inside the sandbox, and catalog/+templates/ are read only by hoop setup running on the host. The image is self-contained — no host repo bind-mount — so it runs on a host with neither the repo at a fixed path nor Claude Code installed. (Set HOOP_PLUGIN_DEV=1 to overlay the full host repo back onto /opt/hoop for live-editing plugin source in development.)
  • dashboard (untrusted view) — Next.js bound to 127.0.0.1:7842, with no claude binary and no access to ~/.claude. Every API route is a thin proxy that calls the sandbox over the socket, so a compromised dashboard can only do what the sandbox API allows.

events.db stays the source of truth: the sandbox writes it (hooks fire there), the dashboard only reads it via the socket. Three tokens gate the three hops:

Token Hop Header
dashboard.token browser ↔ dashboard x-dashboard-token (+ cookie)
sandbox.token dashboard ↔ sandbox x-sandbox-token
hook.token hook scripts ↔ sandbox /ingest x-hook-token

This is the "sandboxed agent" model: the OS-process boundary is the security boundary, and the dashboard holds no secrets. Pairing (co-driving a session with a teammate) layers on top via cloudflared and per-peer share tokens.

State written by the plugin

~/.claude/hoop/
  hoop.env                    opt-in overrides forwarded into the sandbox (0600):
                              OPENAI_API_KEY / EMBEDDING_BASE_URL / EMBEDDING_MODEL /
                              EMBED_DIM / telemetry switches
  host-gateway.cache          cached host.docker.internal address
  shares.json                 active pairing share links
  sandbox/profile/            the sandbox's Claude HOME (bind-mounted to /home/agent)
    .claude/
      .credentials.json       the sandbox's OWN Claude OAuth token (never the host's)
      hoop/
        events.db             SQLite (FTS5 + sqlite-vec); the dashboard reads it over the socket
        events.jsonl          append-only event audit log + replay buffer if the dashboard is down
        install-log.md        audit trail of every `hoop setup` run
        profile.md            identity + installed-stack summary written by the wizard
~/.local/share/hoop/          per-install secrets: dashboard.token, peer-signing.secret (0600)

Because the sandbox's HOME is bind-mounted from sandbox/profile/, everything the container writes to its own ~/.claude/hoop/ lands under that nested path on the host — the agent owns all runtime state; the dashboard only reads it over the socket.

The plugin does not edit your ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md or ~/.claude/settings.json.

Repo structure

hoop/
  .claude-plugin/marketplace.json        self-hosted marketplace
  plugins/hoop/
    .claude-plugin/plugin.json           manifest
    commands/                            /hoop:setup, /hoop:dashboard, /hoop:plan, /hoop:add, /hoop:mount
    catalog/                             8 install recipes (one per wizard layer)
    hooks/scripts/                       emit-event.sh (Unix-socket push, <50ms) + permission-gate.sh
    sandbox/                             trusted agent runtime (owns claude + all state)
      Dockerfile                         sandbox image (claude + Node HTTP server on a UDS)
      server.ts                          HTTP-over-Unix-socket API the dashboard proxies to
      lib/                               active-sessions, db, ingestor, embeddings, sessions,
                                         skills, agents, search, spawn, shares, peer-joins, …
    dashboard/                           untrusted view (no claude, no credentials)
      Dockerfile                         dashboard image (Next.js standalone; no claude, no compilers)
      docker-compose.yml                 agent-sandbox + dashboard services (+ optional DMR embedding model via Compose `models:`)
      app/api/*                          proxy routes → sandbox over the socket
      lib/sandbox-client/                HTTP-over-UDS client; lib/auth*, lib/peer-*  (auth + pairing)
    shared/                              logger, clamp, shutdown (used by both images)
    templates/                           profile.md + install-log.md wizard templates (read host-side)
    cli/                                 oosh-based `hoop` CLI (ships inside the plugin)
      oo.sh                              oosh framework engine
      hoop.sh                            entry point (+ hoop.comp.sh / hoop.zcomp.sh)
      lib/stack.sh                       the two-service runtime engine (preflight + compose)
      modules/                           dashboard, sandbox, open, login, logout, add, mount, setup, doctor, install, uninstall
  README.md
  LICENSE

Hooks pipeline

The sandbox seeds its own profile on boot (sandbox/seed-profile.mjs, run by the container entrypoint using the image's baked Node — no host jq): it wires settings.json hooks on PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SessionStart, Stop, and UserPromptSubmit — declared in the sandbox profile (not in a host hooks.json) so they only ever run inside the sandbox, never on your host. Every event runs hooks/scripts/emit-event.sh which:

  1. POSTs the JSON event to the sandbox's /ingest endpoint over the Unix domain socket (--unix-socket) via curl --max-time 1 — push, not polling. (HOOP_INGEST_URL can override the target for legacy/dev setups.)
  2. Falls back to appending to events.jsonl if the socket isn't reachable.
  3. Exits in <50ms — pure bash + curl, no node/python/jq.

The sandbox is the sole writer: its ingest route persists to SQLite + FTS5 (and sqlite-vec if semantic search is enabled), then emits on an in-process EventEmitter that feeds the SSE stream the dashboard proxies to the browser. On startup, the ingestor drains events.jsonl from its saved offset so events written while the dashboard was down replay automatically.

hooks/scripts/permission-gate.sh (PreToolUse) is the sole tool-permission gate: it asks the sandbox over the same socket and blocks until the host (or a peer allowed to decide) responds — this is what backs /plan's read-only enforcement and the plan-review approval flow below.

Roadmap

  • v0.1 (this release): wizard, containerized dashboard, push-based event pipeline, BM25 + opt-in semantic search.
  • v0.2: inject skill triggers into existing Claude sessions instead of spawning new ones; per-skill-run isolation via ephemeral containers.
  • v0.3+: more catalog entries; non-Claude clients (Cursor, Codex) where MCPs overlap.

Contributing

This is opinionated by design. PRs welcome, especially:

  • Verifying install commands on less-common platforms (Windows, NixOS).
  • Adding new catalog options with verified install recipes.

Please open an issue before changing the curation philosophy (curated menus, one-consent install, no re-implementation, single-user localhost dashboard).

License

MIT

(optional) Configure your stack interactively — memory, code-graph RAG, # platform MCPs, docs RAG, semantic search, observability, second-brain, etc. # This is the /hoop:setup wizard, native to the CLI (needs no host Claude Code). hoop setup # 5. One-time: authenticate the sandbox with its OWN Claude account. # Drops you into `claude` inside the sandbox — type /login, open the printed # URL in your browser, approve, paste the code back, then /exit. hoop login Then open http://localhost:7842/. Because the dashboard only trusts localhost, your browser is recognized as the host automatically — the access token is minted for you, nothing to paste. (Remote access is deliberately gated; see Architecture.) That's the full loop: hoop start → hoop setup (optional) → hoop login → open the dashboard. A few notes for the standalone path: Configure your stack with hoop setup. This is the interactive wizard (memory, code-graph RAG, automation, platform MCPs, docs RAG, semantic search, observability, design, second-brain, telemetry isolation) — the same one behind /hoop:setup, but native to the CLI so it needs no host Claude Code. It runs before hoop login (writing MCP config needs no auth). For one-off additions later, hoop add mcp/plugin/skill (see the CLI table) persists into the sandbox profile across rebuilds. Terminal-only? hoop open runs an interactive claude in a throwaway sandbox over your current directory — no dashboard, no browser needed after login. Updating: git pull then hoop rebuild picks up new code into both images. Signing out / switching accounts: hoop logout clears the sandbox's Claude login so a different account can hoop login. What the wizard does hoop setup (or /hoop:setup, which points you to it) walks you through these steps with one consent at the top, then installs each pick — auto-runnable and secret-taking MCPs run immediately (every command printed first); browser-login, plugin-marketplace, and host-CLI options are printed as guided steps to finish yourself: # Step Pick 1 Consent Y / N 2 Detect prior state (read-only) 3 Memory claude-mem (installed automatically — no choice) 4 Code-graph RAG (if you code) Serena / claude-context / code-graph-mcp / Cognee / skip 5 Automation n8n-mcp yes / no 6 Platform MCPs multi-select: Atlassian, Google Workspace, GitHub, incident.io, Slack 7 Docs RAG Context7 yes / no 8 Observability Sentry / Datadog (multi-select) 9 Design Excalidraw yes / no 10 Second-brain Obsidian (3 flavors) / Notion (2) / Logseq / NotebookLM 11 Sign-ins Claude Code /login, then gh (device flow) + any queued MCP OAuth — all inside the sandbox Each run appends to the sandbox profile's ~/.claude/hoop/sandbox/profile/.claude/hoop/install-log.md (also viewable from the dashboard) so re-runs are auditable. Secrets never reach the log — they go straight to claude mcp add -e or the 0600 ~/.claude/hoop/hoop.env. Dashboard /hoop:dashboard (or start | stop | restart | rebuild | status | logs) runs the dashboard inside a container. Your host only needs Docker Desktop — no Node, no npm install, no Next.js build pollution. Each verb takes an optional service target (all (default) · sandbox · dashboard); start builds lazily (only when an image is missing) while rebuild always rebuilds. Pairing (inviting a teammate to co-drive a session) uses cloudflared to expose the local dashboard over a public tunnel — it's baked into the dashboard image, so nothing to install on the host. The dashboard runs fine without pairing; only share links start a tunnel. Five panels: Sessions — fs.watch on ~/.claude/sessions/; updates in real time. Skills — every skill on disk (user + plugin), filterable, with a one-click "Run" that spawns claude -p '/<name>' inside the dashboard container and streams stdout back to the panel. Sub-agents — nested tree reconstructed from PreToolUse / PostToolUse events on the Agent tool. Click a node to see its prompt, tool calls, and final output. Events — chronological live tail via Server-Sent Events. Hooks push each event to the sandbox's /ingest over the Unix domain socket; the dashboard tails the resulting stream with zero polling. Search — opens with ⌘K. BM25 (FTS5) always works; semantic search (sqlite-vec, 768-dim embeddings) activates when an embedding backend is configured. Set one up via hoop setup / /hoop:setup — recommended is Docker Model Runner (added to the compose stack via Compose's models: element and pulled on hoop start), with Ollama (nomic-embed-text), OpenAI, or any custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint as alternatives. Hybrid fuses BM25 + semantic via Reciprocal Rank Fusion. The dashboard is single-user and localhost-only by design; access is gated by a per-install token (see Architecture below). CLI (hoop) plugins/hoop/cli/ ships a small oosh-based CLI that wraps the runtime. It lives inside the plugin (framework engine + entry point + completions + the stack engine in lib/stack.sh) so it ships with the plugin and the slash commands (/hoop:setup, /hoop:dashboard) can invoke it directly. It needs no external install and resolves its own paths. ./plugins/hoop/cli/hoop.sh install # symlink `hoop` onto PATH + shell completion (bash/zsh) # or run in place without installing: ./plugins/hoop/cli/hoop.sh <module> <command> Two levels: top-level verbs act on the whole stack; modules scope a single service. All of them drive one engine (cli/lib/stack.sh) — the single source of truth for host-side preflight (profile bind-mount prep, dashboard/peer auth tokens, forwarded embedding env, compose orchestration). The Claude onboarding bypass + plugin/hook wiring run inside the sandbox on boot (sandbox/seed-profile.mjs), so the host needs no jq. start/rebuild are deliberately split — start only builds an image when it's missing (fast otherwise), while rebuild always rebuilds so you pick up code changes. Command What it does hoop start · stop · restart · rebuild · status · logs Whole stack (agent-sandbox + dashboard) via the engine (up -d / down / build + up -d --force-recreate for the project). rebuild takes -n|--no-cache. hoop login · logout Authenticate the sandbox with its own Claude account (one-time). login drops you into claude inside the running sandbox to run /login (paste-code flow — approve the URL in your host browser); the sandbox mints and self-refreshes its own OAuth token. logout clears it so you can sign in as a different account. Your host credentials are never read or copied. Module Commands What it does dashboard start · stop · restart · rebuild · status · logs Controls only the dashboard UI container (--no-deps, leaves agent-sandbox alone). rebuild takes -n|--no-cache. sandbox start · stop · restart · rebuild · update Controls only the agent-sandbox container. Lifecycle verbs run the shared engine scoped to the sandbox (so plugin wiring + forwarded env still happen); rebuild recreates the container (-n|--no-cache to skip the layer cache); update pins the baked-in claude-code version (-c|--claude-version). open (default) Runs a fresh, telemetry-isolated sandbox over the current directory: mounts $PWD read-write into the agent workspace and launches claude interactively (docker run --rm -it, real tty for the TUI). Forces HOOP_DISABLE_TELEMETRY=1 (add -T|--telemetry to allow bundled-tool telemetry) and strips the dashboard-only hooks + the hoop plugin from an overlay settings.json, while keeping credentials, setup MCPs, skills, and other plugins. Extra args pass straight through, e.g. hoop open --model opus or hoop open "fix the failing test". add mcp · plugin · skill Installs a component into the sandbox profile so it persists across rebuild/restart/recreate and is shared by both dashboard sessions and hoop open (the profile is bind-mounted into both). mcp <name> [flags] [-- <cmd>] → claude mcp add, defaulting to --scope user so the server is global (not stranded under one project — pass your own -s|--scope to override); plugin [-m|--marketplace <spec>] <plugin[@marketplace]> → claude plugin install; skill -d|--dir <dir> [-f|--force] copies a local skill directory (must contain SKILL.md) into ~/.claude/skills/<name>, dereferencing symlinks so the real files resolve inside the container. mcp/plugin need the sandbox running; skill is host-only. mount add · list · remove Bind-mounts host folders into the sandbox workspace at /home/agent/workspace/<name>. add -p|--path <host-dir> [-n|--name <name>] adds one (the -p path tab-completes directories), list shows the configured mounts, remove <name> drops one. Mounts persist via a generated compose override; each add/remove recreates the agent-sandbox container. install (default) Bare hoop install symlinks hoop onto PATH + wires shell completion (-f|--force reinstalls over an existing symlink). The repo itself is never modified; hoop uninstall removes the symlink and shell wiring. setup (default) Runs the interactive stack wizard (the native port of /hoop:setup): boots the sandbox, then walks memory / code-graph / automation / platform MCPs / docs RAG / semantic search / observability / design / second-brain / telemetry, installing each pick into the sandbox profile. Auto/secret MCPs run via claude mcp add; OAuth/plugin/host-CLI sign-ins complete at the end inside the sandbox. Needs a TTY (refuses head-less), runs before hoop login, writes profile.md + install-log.md. --reset-first wipes all sandbox state for a blank slate first. doctor (default) Read-only health check of the host + stack through a Docker-only-standalone lens: verifies Docker + Compose v2, reports which optional subcommand tools (jq/curl/awk) are present, checks the sandbox image/containers, Claude auth, and the semantic-search backend (incl. the DMR Compose models: override + reachability). Fails only on truly blocking problems; everything else is advisory. hoop start # bring up the whole stack at http://localhost:7842/ hoop setup # interactive wizard: configure the sandbox stack (no host Claude Code needed) hoop login # one-time: authenticate the sandbox with its own Claude account hoop dashboard rebuild # rebuild + recreate ONLY the dashboard container hoop sandbox rebuild # rebuild + recreate ONLY the agent-sandbox container hoop open # interactive claude in a sandbox over $PWD hoop add mcp context7 -- npx -y @upstash/context7-mcp # install an MCP (user scope) into the sandbox hoop add skill -d ~/.claude/skills/impeccable # copy a local skill into the sandbox profile hoop mount add -p ~/code/myproject # expose a host folder to the sandbox workspace The add / mount subcommands are also exposed as the /hoop:add and /hoop:mount slash commands, which delegate to this CLI. Everything add writes lives in ~/.claude/hoop/sandbox/profile (bind-mounted at /home/agent), so a component installed once is available in every dashboard session and in hoop open, and survives image rebuilds. hoop open uses the hoop-sandbox image (build it once with hoop sandbox rebuild) and mounts the sandbox Claude profile (~/.claude/hoop/sandbox/profile, -p\|--profile to override) so claude is already authenticated — run hoop login once to authenticate the sandbox with its own Claude account. Unlike the dashboard's agent-sandbox, open runs with telemetry blackholed and without hoop's dashboard hooks/plugin (which need the dashboard's socket), so it's a clean, isolated interactive session that still has your setup MCPs and skills. hoop install / hoop uninstall manage the PATH symlink and shell wiring; the repo itself is never modified. Browser automation The hoop-sandbox image ships a headless Chromium + the official @playwright/mcp, registered in the sandbox profile automatically (from inside the container, via claude mcp add, so the registration can't point at a browser the running image doesn't have). The agent gets browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_type, browser_fill_form, browser_snapshot, browser_take_screenshot, and more — driven by a browser that runs entirely in-container as the unprivileged agent user. No host browser, no host process, no host.docker.internal gymnastics, and nothing runs in your user context. Because the same image + profile back both surfaces, browser tools are available in every dashboard session and in hoop open. Two deliberate hardening choices: Ephemeral profile (--isolated). The browser profile is kept in memory and never written to disk, so no cookies or logins persist across sessions and there's no ambient auth lying around. To drive a site that needs a login, hand the browser tools a --storage-state file (see the @playwright/mcp docs). No arbitrary-code tool. @playwright/mcp exposes browser_run_code_unsafe (arbitrary JavaScript in the Playwright process, RCE-equivalent) as a non-removable "core" capability. hoop denies it via Claude's own permissions.deny in the sandbox settings.json, so the model never sees it — the rest of the toolset is unaffected. Pairing & plan review /hoop:dashboard can hand a share link to a teammate over a cloudflared tunnel. They open it, pick a name, and the host admits them; from that point both sides see the same live transcript and can chat (> prefix) or co-drive the model — from a laptop or a phone. Run a turn with /plan <task> and the sandbox forces the agent read-only: it investigates, then submits a plan that opens in a review panel. The host and any full-capability peer can drop inline comments anchored to the exact passage — synced live across everyone — then Approve or Request changes. A rejection feeds the comments back and the agent revises the plan. The host's flow, end to end 1 · Chat — a peer joins and both sides chat in-thread (> prefix); no model turn is spent. 2 · Prompt — the host sends a normal turn to the model. 3 · Clarify — the agent asks structured questions before planning. 4 · Observe — every hook event streams into the live Events panel. 5 · Plan — /plan forces the agent read-only; it drafts a plan instead of acting. 6 · Review — the plan opens for inline comments, then Approve / Request changes. 7 · Iterate — “Request changes” feeds the comments back; the agent revises read-only and resubmits, and once the host approves the session leaves plan mode and implements it. Peers co-drive the same session from anywhere — here's “Rafael” reviewing the plan from his phone: Architecture The runtime is split across two containers so a compromise of the web layer can't reach your credentials: agent-sandbox (trusted) — owns the claude binary, your Claude profile (OAuth credentials, plugins, MCP config, sessions/transcripts), the long-lived claude -p --input-format=stream-json subprocesses, and events.db (sole writer). It exposes a small HTTP API over a Unix domain socket (/var/run/hoop/sandbox.sock) — no TCP port. The model runs as an unprivileged agent user, and the claude-facing plugin surface the sandbox actually uses (hook scripts + the .mcp.json tools server + the plugin manifest) is baked into the sandbox image at /opt/hoop, root-owned so that user can't tamper with the hook scripts. Host-only pieces are deliberately not baked: the commands/ (host-stack slash commands) and agents/ (host diagnostics) aren't meaningful inside the sandbox, and catalog/+templates/ are read only by hoop setup running on the host. The image is self-contained — no host repo bind-mount — so it runs on a host with neither the repo at a fixed path nor Claude Code installed. (Set HOOP_PLUGIN_DEV=1 to overlay the full host repo back onto /opt/hoop for live-editing plugin source in development.) dashboard (untrusted view) — Next.js bound to 127.0.0.1:7842, with no claude binary and no access to ~/.claude. Every API route is a thin proxy that calls the sandbox over the socket, so a compromised dashboard can only do what the sandbox API allows. events.db stays the source of truth: the sandbox writes it (hooks fire there), the dashboard only reads it via the socket. Three tokens gate the three hops: Token Hop Header dashboard.token browser ↔ dashboard x-dashboard-token (+ cookie) sandbox.token dashboard ↔ sandbox x-sandbox-token hook.token hook scripts ↔ sandbox /ingest x-hook-token This is the "sandboxed agent" model: the OS-process boundary is the security boundary, and the dashboard holds no secrets. Pairing (co-driving a session with a teammate) layers on top via cloudflared and per-peer share tokens. State written by the plugin ~/.claude/hoop/ hoop.env opt-in overrides forwarded into the sandbox (0600): OPENAI_API_KEY / EMBEDDING_BASE_URL / EMBEDDING_MODEL / EMBED_DIM / telemetry switches host-gateway.cache cached host.docker.internal address shares.json active pairing share links sandbox/profile/ the sandbox's Claude HOME (bind-mounted to /home/agent) .claude/ .credentials.json the sandbox's OWN Claude OAuth token (never the host's) hoop/ events.db SQLite (FTS5 + sqlite-vec); the dashboard reads it over the socket events.jsonl append-only event audit log + replay buffer if the dashboard is down install-log.md audit trail of every `hoop setup` run profile.md identity + installed-stack summary written by the wizard ~/.local/share/hoop/ per-install secrets: dashboard.token, peer-signing.secret (0600) Because the sandbox's HOME is bind-mounted from sandbox/profile/, everything the container writes to its own ~/.claude/hoop/ lands under that nested path on the host — the agent owns all runtime state; the dashboard only reads it over the socket. The plugin does not edit your ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md or ~/.claude/settings.json. Repo structure hoop/ .claude-plugin/marketplace.json self-hosted marketplace plugins/hoop/ .claude-plugin/plugin.json manifest commands/ /hoop:setup, /hoop:dashboard, /hoop:plan, /hoop:add, /hoop:mount catalog/ 8 install recipes (one per wizard layer) hooks/scripts/ emit-event.sh (Unix-socket push, <50ms) + permission-gate.sh sandbox/ trusted agent runtime (owns claude + all state) Dockerfile sandbox image (claude + Node HTTP server on a UDS) server.ts HTTP-over-Unix-socket API the dashboard proxies to lib/ active-sessions, db, ingestor, embeddings, sessions, skills, agents, search, spawn, shares, peer-joins, … dashboard/ untrusted view (no claude, no credentials) Dockerfile dashboard image (Next.js standalone; no claude, no compilers) docker-compose.yml agent-sandbox + dashboard services (+ optional DMR embedding model via Compose `models:`) app/api/* proxy routes → sandbox over the socket lib/sandbox-client/ HTTP-over-UDS client; lib/auth*, lib/peer-* (auth + pairing) shared/ logger, clamp, shutdown (used by both images) templates/ profile.md + install-log.md wizard templates (read host-side) cli/ oosh-based `hoop` CLI (ships inside the plugin) oo.sh oosh framework engine hoop.sh entry point (+ hoop.comp.sh / hoop.zcomp.sh) lib/stack.sh the two-service runtime engine (preflight + compose) modules/ dashboard, sandbox, open, login, logout, add, mount, setup, doctor, install, uninstall README.md LICENSE Hooks pipeline The sandbox seeds its own profile on boot (sandbox/seed-profile.mjs, run by the container entrypoint using the image's baked Node — no host jq): it wires settings.json hooks on PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SessionStart, Stop, and UserPromptSubmit — declared in the sandbox profile (not in a host hooks.json) so they only ever run inside the sandbox, never on your host. Every event runs hooks/scripts/emit-event.sh which: POSTs the JSON event to the sandbox's /ingest endpoint over the Unix domain socket (--unix-socket) via curl --max-time 1 — push, not polling. (HOOP_INGEST_URL can override the target for legacy/dev setups.) Falls back to appending to events.jsonl if the socket isn't reachable. Exits in <50ms — pure bash + curl, no node/python/jq. The sandbox is the sole writer: its ingest route persists to SQLite + FTS5 (and sqlite-vec if semantic search is enabled), then emits on an in-process EventEmitter that feeds the SSE stream the dashboard proxies to the browser. On startup, the ingestor drains events.jsonl from its saved offset so events written while the dashboard was down replay automatically. hooks/scripts/permission-gate.sh (PreToolUse) is the sole tool-permission gate: it asks the sandbox over the same socket and blocks until the host (or a peer allowed to decide) responds — this is what backs /plan's read-only enforcement and the plan-review approval flow below. Roadmap v0.1 (this release): wizard, containerized dashboard, push-based event pipeline, BM25 + opt-in semantic search. v0.2: inject skill triggers into existing Claude sessions instead of spawning new ones; per-skill-run isolation via ephemeral containers. v0.3+: more catalog entries; non-Claude clients (Cursor, Codex) where MCPs overlap. Contributing This is opinionated by design. PRs welcome, especially: Verifying install commands on less-common platforms (Windows, NixOS). Adding new catalog options with verified install recipes. Please open an issue before changing the curation philosophy (curated menus, one-consent install, no re-implementation, single-user localhost dashboard). License MIT

blocks.html_en: • Docker Model Runner (optional) — for semantic-search embeddings. hoop setup enables it (Docker Desktop AI, or the docker-model-plugin on Docker Engine) and appends the embedding model to the compose stack via Compose's models: element (Docker Compose v2.38+), so hoop start auto-pulls it and wires the endpoint into the sandbox. If DMR is ever unreachable — or Compose is older than v2.38 — hoop start transparently drops the override and falls back to BM25 rather than failing. Skippable — Ollama, OpenAI, or a custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint also work.

sentences.en: Docker Model Runner (optional) — for semantic-search embeddings.

sentences.en: hoop setup enables it (Docker Desktop AI, or the docker-model-plugin on Docker Engine) and appends the embedding model to the compose stack via Compose's models: element (Docker Compose v2.38+), so hoop start auto-pulls it and wires the endpoint into the sandbox.

blocks.html_en: /hoop:dashboard (or start | stop | restart | rebuild | status | logs) runs the dashboard inside a container. Your host only needs Docker Desktop — no Node, no npm install, no Next.js build pollution. Each verb takes an optional service target (all (default) · sandbox · dashboard); start builds lazily (only when an image is missing) while rebuild always rebuilds.

Hybrid fuses BM25 + semantic via Reciprocal Rank Fusion.

blocks.html_en: plugins/hoop/cli/ ships a small oosh-based CLI that wraps the runtime. It lives inside the plugin (framework engine + entry point + completions + the stack engine in lib/stack.sh) so it ships with the plugin and the slash commands (/hoop:setup, /hoop:dashboard) can invoke it directly. It needs no external install and resolves its own paths.

sentences.en: plugins/hoop/cli/ ships a small oosh-based CLI that wraps the runtime.
sentences.zh: plugins/hoop/cli/ 提供了一个基于 oosh 的小型 CLI，它包装了运行时。

sentences.en: It lives inside the plugin (framework engine + entry point + completions + the stack engine in lib/stack.sh) so it ships with the plugin and the slash commands (/hoop:setup, /hoop:dashboard) can invoke it directly.
sentences.zh: 它位于插件内部（框架引擎 + 入口点 + 完成 + lib/stack.sh 中的堆栈引擎），因此它随插件一起提供，并且斜杠命令（/hoop:setup、/hoop:dashboard）可以直接调用它。

sentences.en: It needs no external install and resolves its own paths.
sentences.zh: 它不需要外部安装并解析自己的路径。

blocks.html_en: Two levels: top-level verbs act on the whole stack; modules scope a single service. All of them drive one engine (cli/lib/stack.sh) — the single source of truth for host-side preflight (profile bind-mount prep, dashboard/peer auth tokens, forwarded embedding env, compose orchestration). The Claude onboarding bypass + plugin/hook wiring run inside the sandbox on boot (sandbox/seed-profile.mjs), so the host needs no jq. start/rebuild are deliberately split — start only builds an image when it's missing (fast otherwise), while rebuild always rebuilds so you pick up code changes.

sentences.en: Two levels: top-level verbs act on the whole stack; modules scope a single service.
sentences.zh: 两级：顶级动词作用于整个堆栈；模块范围仅限于单一服务。

sentences.en: All of them drive one engine (cli/lib/stack.sh) — the single source of truth for host-side preflight (profile bind-mount prep, dashboard/peer auth tokens, forwarded embedding env, compose orchestration).
sentences.zh: 它们都驱动一个引擎 (cli/lib/stack.sh) — 主机端预检的单一事实来源（配置文件绑定安装准备、仪表板/对等身份验证令牌、转发嵌入环境、撰写编排）。

sentences.en: The Claude onboarding bypass + plugin/hook wiring run inside the sandbox on boot (sandbox/seed-profile.mjs), so the host needs no jq.
sentences.zh: Claude 引导旁路 + 插件/挂钩接线在启动时在沙箱内运行 (sandbox/seed-profile.mjs)，因此主机不需要 jq。

sentences.en: start/rebuild are deliberately split — start only builds an image when it's missing (fast otherwise), while rebuild always rebuilds so you pick up code changes.
sentences.zh: 启动/重建是故意分开的——启动仅在图像丢失时构建图像（否则很快），而重建总是重建，以便您拾取代码更改。

blocks.html_en: Authenticate the sandbox with its own Claude account (one-time). login drops you into claude inside the running sandbox to run /login (paste-code flow — approve the URL in your host browser); the sandbox mints and self-refreshes its own OAuth token. logout clears it so you can sign in as a different account. Your host credentials are never read or copied.

(default)

blocks.html_en: Runs a fresh, telemetry-isolated sandbox over the current directory: mounts $PWD read-write into the agent workspace and launches claude interactively (docker run --rm -it, real tty for the TUI). Extra args pass straight through, e.g. hoop open --model opus or hoop open "fix the failing test".

blocks.html_en: add
mcp · plugin · skill

sentences.en: mcp · plugin · skill
sentences.zh: mcp·插件·技能

blocks.html_en: Installs a component into the sandbox profile so it persists across rebuild/restart/recreate and is shared by both dashboard sessions and hoop open (the profile is bind-mounted into both). mcp <name> [flags] [-- <cmd>] → claude mcp add, defaulting to --scope user so the server is global (not stranded under one project — pass your own -s|--scope to override); plugin [-m|--marketplace <spec>] <plugin[@marketplace]> → claude plugin install; skill -d|--dir <dir> [-f|--force] copies a local skill directory (must contain SKILL.md) into ~/.claude/skills/<name>, dereferencing symlinks so the real files resolve inside the container. mcp/plugin need the sandbox running; skill is host-only.

sentences.en: Installs a component into the sandbox profile so it persists across rebuild/restart/recreate and is shared by both dashboard sessions and hoop open (the profile is bind-mounted into both).

sentences.en: mcp <name> [flags] [-- <cmd>] → claude mcp add, defaulting to --scope user so the server is global (not stranded under one project — pass your own -s|--scope to override); plugin [-m|--marketplace <spec>] <plugin[@marketplace]> → claude plugin install; skill -d|--dir <dir> [-f|--force] copies a local skill directory (must contain SKILL.md) into ~/.claude/skills/<name>, dereferencing symlinks so the real files resolve inside the container.
sentences.zh: mcp <name> [flags] [-- <cmd>] → claude mcp add，默认为 --scope 用户，因此服务器是全局的（不会滞留在一个项目下 - 传递您自己的 -s|--scope 进行覆盖）；插件 [-m|--marketplace <spec>] <plugin[@marketplace]> → claude 插件安装； Skill -d|--dir <dir> [-f|--force] 将本地技能目录（必须包含 SKILL.md）复制到 ~/.claude/skills/<name> 中，取消引用符号链接，以便真正的文件在容器内解析。

blocks.html_en: Browser automation

sentences.en: Browser automation
sentences.zh: 浏览器自动化

blocks.html_en: The hoop-sandbox image ships a headless Chromium + the official @playwright/mcp, registered in the sandbox profile automatically (from inside the container, via claude mcp add, so the registration can't point at a browser the running image doesn't have). The agent gets browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_type, browser_fill_form, browser_snapshot, browser_take_screenshot, and more — driven by a browser that runs entirely in-container as the unprivileged agent user. No host browser, no host process, no host.docker.internal gymnastics, and nothing runs in your user context. Because the same image + profile back both surfaces, browser tools are available in every dashboard session and in hoop open.

sentences.en: The hoop-sandbox image ships a headless Chromium + the official @playwright/mcp, registered in the sandbox profile automatically (from inside the container, via claude mcp add, so the registration can't point at a browser the running image doesn't have).
sentences.zh: hoop-sandbox 镜像附带一个无头 Chromium + 官方 @playwright/mcp，自动在沙盒配置文件中注册（从容器内部，通过 claude mcp add，因此注册不能指向正在运行的镜像没有的浏览器）。

sentences.en: The agent gets browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_type, browser_fill_form, browser_snapshot, browser_take_screenshot, and more — driven by a browser that runs entirely in-container as the unprivileged agent user.
sentences.zh: 代理获取 browser_navigate、browser_click、browser_type、browser_fill_form、browser_snapshot、browser_take_screenshot 等，由完全在容器内以非特权代理用户身份运行的浏览器驱动。

sentences.en: No host browser, no host process, no host.docker.internal gymnastics, and nothing runs in your user context.
sentences.zh: 没有主机浏览器，没有主机进程，没有 host.docker.internal 体操，并且用户上下文中没有任何东西运行。

sentences.en: Because the same image + profile back both surfaces, browser tools are available in every dashboard session and in hoop open.
sentences.zh: 由于两个表面支持相同的图像和配置文件，因此浏览器工具在每个仪表板会话和打开的环中都可用。

blocks.html_en: Two deliberate hardening choices:

sentences.en: Two deliberate hardening choices:
sentences.zh: 两种刻意的强化选择：

blocks.html_en: • Ephemeral profile (--isolated). The browser profile is kept in memory and never written to disk, so no cookies or logins persist across sessions and there's no ambient auth lying around. To drive a site that needs a login, hand the browser tools a --storage-state file (see the @playwright/mcp docs).

sentences.en: Ephemeral profile (--isolated).
sentences.zh: 短暂的轮廓（--隔离）。

sentences.en: The browser profile is kept in memory and never written to disk, so no cookies or logins persist across sessions and there's no ambient auth lying around.
sentences.zh: 浏览器配置文件保存在内存中，并且永远不会写入磁盘，因此会话之间不会保留任何 cookie 或登录信息，也没有周围的环境身份验证。

sentences.en: To drive a site that needs a login, hand the browser tools a --storage-state file (see the @playwright/mcp docs).
sentences.zh: 要驱动需要登录的站点，请向浏览器工具提供一个 --storage-state 文件（请参阅 @playwright/mcp 文档）。

blocks.html_en: • No arbitrary-code tool. @playwright/mcp exposes browser_run_code_unsafe (arbitrary JavaScript in the Playwright process, RCE-equivalent) as a non-removable "core" capability. hoop denies it via Claude's own permissions.deny in the sandbox settings.json, so the model never sees it — the rest of the toolset is unaffected.

sentences.en: No arbitrary-code tool. @playwright/mcp exposes browser_run_code_unsafe (arbitrary JavaScript in the Playwright process, RCE-equivalent) as a non-removable "core" capability.
sentences.zh: 没有任意代码工具。 @playwright/mcp 将 browser_run_code_unsafe （Playwright 进程中的任意 JavaScript，RCE 等效项）公开为不可移除的“核心”功能。

sentences.en: hoop denies it via Claude's own permissions.deny in the sandbox settings.json, so the model never sees it — the rest of the toolset is unaffected.
sentences.zh: hoop 通过沙箱 settings.json 中 Claude 自己的 Permissions.deny 拒绝它，因此模型永远不会看到它 - 工具集的其余部分不受影响。

blocks.html_en: Pairing & plan review

sentences.en: Pairing & plan review
sentences.zh: 配对和计划审查

blocks.html_en: /hoop:dashboard can hand a share link to a teammate over a cloudflared tunnel. They open it, pick a name, and the host admits them; from that point both sides see the same live transcript and can chat (> prefix) or co-drive the model — from a laptop or a phone.

sentences.zh: 他们打开门，选一个名字，主人就会接纳他们；从那时起，双方都会看到相同的实时记录，并且可以通过笔记本电脑或手机聊天（>前缀）或共同驱动模型。

blocks.html_en: Run a turn with /plan <task> and the sandbox forces the agent read-only: it investigates, then submits a plan that opens in a review panel. The host and any full-capability peer can drop inline comments anchored to the exact passage — synced live across everyone — then Approve or Request changes. A rejection feeds the comments back and the agent revises the plan.

sentences.en: Run a turn with /plan <task> and the sandbox forces the agent read-only: it investigates, then submits a plan that opens in a review panel.

sentences.en: A rejection feeds the comments back and the agent revises the plan.
sentences.zh: 拒绝会反馈意见，代理商会修改计划。

blocks.html_en: The runtime is split across two containers so a compromise of the web layer can't reach your credentials:

sentences.en: The runtime is split across two containers so a compromise of the web layer can't reach your credentials:
sentences.zh: 运行时分为两个容器，因此 Web 层的妥协无法到达您的凭据：

blocks.html_en: • agent-sandbox (trusted) — owns the claude binary, your Claude profile (OAuth credentials, plugins, MCP config, sessions/transcripts), the long-lived claude -p --input-format=stream-json subprocesses, and events.db (sole writer). It exposes a small HTTP API over a Unix domain socket (/var/run/hoop/sandbox.sock) — no TCP port. The model runs as an unprivileged agent user, and the claude-facing plugin surface the sandbox actually uses (hook scripts + the .mcp.json tools server + the plugin manifest) is baked into the sandbox image at /opt/hoop, root-owned so that user can't tamper with the hook scripts. Host-only pieces are deliberately not baked: the commands/ (host-stack slash commands) and agents/ (host diagnostics) aren't meaningful inside the sandbox, and catalog/+templates/ are read only by hoop setup running on the host. The image is self-contained — no host repo bind-mount — so it runs on a host with neither the repo at a fixed path nor Claude Code installed. (Set HOOP_PLUGIN_DEV=1 to overlay the full host repo back onto /opt/hoop for live-editing plugin source in development.)

sentences.en: agent-sandbox (trusted) — owns the claude binary, your Claude profile (OAuth credentials, plugins, MCP config, sessions/transcripts), the long-lived claude -p --input-format=stream-json subprocesses, and events.db (sole writer).
sentences.zh: agent-sandbox（受信任）- 拥有 claude 二进制文件、您的 Claude 配置文件（OAuth 凭证、插件、MCP 配置、会话/脚本）、长期存在的 claude -p --input-format=stream-json 子进程和 events.db（唯一编写者）。

sentences.en: It exposes a small HTTP API over a Unix domain socket (/var/run/hoop/sandbox.sock) — no TCP port.
sentences.zh: 它通过 Unix 域套接字 (/var/run/hoop/sandbox.sock) 公开一个小型 HTTP API — 无 TCP 端口。

sentences.en: The model runs as an unprivileged agent user, and the claude-facing plugin surface the sandbox actually uses (hook scripts + the .mcp.json tools server + the plugin manifest) is baked into the sandbox image at /opt/hoop, root-owned so that user can't tamper with the hook scripts.
sentences.zh: 该模型作为非特权代理用户运行，沙箱实际使用的面向 claude 的插件表面（钩子脚本 + .mcp.json 工具服务器 + 插件清单）被烘焙到位于 /opt/hoop 的沙箱映像中，由 root 拥有，以便用户无法篡改钩子脚本。

sentences.en: Host-only pieces are deliberately not baked: the commands/ (host-stack slash commands) and agents/ (host diagnostics) aren't meaningful inside the sandbox, and catalog/+templates/ are read only by hoop setup running on the host.
sentences.zh: 故意不烘焙仅主机部分：commands/（主机堆栈斜线命令）和agents/（主机诊断）在沙箱内没有意义，而catalog/+templates/只能由主机上运行的hoop设置读取。

sentences.en: The image is self-contained — no host repo bind-mount — so it runs on a host with neither the repo at a fixed path nor Claude Code installed.
sentences.zh: 该映像是独立的 - 没有主机存储库绑定安装 - 因此它运行在既没有位于固定路径的存储库也没有安装 Claude Code 的主机上。

Every event runs hooks/scripts/emit-event.sh which:

sentences.en: The sandbox seeds its own profile on boot (sandbox/seed-profile.mjs, run by the container entrypoint using the image's baked Node — no host jq): it wires settings.json hooks on PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SessionStart, Stop, and UserPromptSubmit — declared in the sandbox profile (not in a host hooks.json) so they only ever run inside the sandbox, never on your host.

On startup, the ingestor drains events.jsonl from its saved offset so events written while the dashboard was down replay automatically.

content_html_zh:
hoop-showcase.mp4

独自一人并不是必要条件。 Claude Code 之上的沙盒和网络工具。 一次安装可交付两个可交付成果：

  1. /hoop:setup — 一个交互式向导，用于连接策划的堆栈（内存、代码图 RAG、自动化、平台 MCP、文档 RAG、可观察性、设计、第二大脑）。
  2. /hoop:dashboard — 一个容器化的本地 Web 仪表板（Next.js，http://localhost:7842/），具有实时会话、具有一键触发器的技能浏览器、嵌套的子代理树、基于推送的事件可观察性以及跨所有事件的 BM25 + 可选语义搜索。

该插件不会重新实现其安装的任何第三方 MCP 或技能。 它挑选、安装、记录并观察它们。

安装

有两种方法进去。 选择与您的主人相匹配的一个。

A · Inside Claude Code（你已经运行过 Claude Code）

/plugin marketplace add bruno-de-queiroz/hoop
/plugin install hoop@hoop-marketplace
/plugin list
/reload-plugins
/hoop:setup

（Claude Code v2.1.138 需要 /plugin list + /reload-plugins dance 才能在当前会话中激活新预先植入的插件。 新会话不需要它。）

B · 独立 — 主机上没有 Claude 代码

您不需要在主机上安装 Claude Code（甚至 Node）。 沙箱提供自己的 claude 二进制文件并使用自己的 Claude 帐户进行身份验证，因此带有 Docker 的裸机就足够了。

先决条件

  • Docker — Docker Desktop，或任何 Docker 引擎 + Compose v2 (docker compose)。 这是 hoop start 的唯一硬依赖项 — Claude Code、Node、gh、uv、gws 和仪表板都位于容器中，而不是在您的主机上，并且沙箱在启动时播种其自己的 Claude 配置文件（因此 hoop start 不需要主机 jq）。 其他一些子命令确实可以使用小型主机工具 - 请参阅下面的可选列表。
  • git 和 bash — 克隆存储库并运行 CLI。
  • 您可以访问的网络浏览器 - 用于一次性登录批准。 它不必在同一台机器上；您只需打开一个 URL 并将代码复制回来（参见步骤 4）。 对于无头服务器来说很方便。
  • 具有有效计划（Pro / Max / Team / Enterprise）的 Claude 帐户，可以通过 /login 登录。
  • jq（某些子命令需要，而不是 hoop start） - hoop 设置和 hoop 注销需要，它在主机上编辑 JSON（向导将您的 gh/Claude 身份读取到 profile.md 中；注销会重写沙箱凭据）。 hoop open 也使用它，但如果不存在，则会降级并发出警告。 安装：macOSbrew install jq； Debian/Ubuntu sudo apt-get install -y jq。
  • curl（可选——从不严格要求）——由hoop setup用来探测Docker Model Runner和仪表板健康等待；当它丢失时，两者都会回退到 bash /dev/tcp 探针。 安装：brew安装curl / sudo apt-get install -ycurl。
  • awk（仅用于箍挂载）- 箍挂载添加/删除需要重写挂载列表；被所有其他命令未使用。
  • Docker Model Runner（可选）——用于语义搜索嵌入。 hoop setup 启用它（Docker Desktop AI，或 Docker Engine 上的 docker-model-plugin），并通过 Compose 的 models: element (Docker Compose v2.38+) 将嵌入模型附加到 compose 堆栈，因此 hoop start 自动拉取它并将端点连接到沙箱中。 如果 DMR 无法访问，或者 Compose 版本早于 v2.38，hoop start 会透明地放弃覆盖并回退到 BM25，而不是失败。 可跳过 — Ollama、OpenAI 或自定义 OpenAI 兼容端点也可以使用。

随时运行 hoop doctor 来验证主机是否拥有一切，并查看距离干净的仅 Docker 设置有多近。

Steps

# 1. Clone and enter the repo
git clone https://github.com/bruno-de-queiroz/hoop && cd hoop

# 2. (optional) Put `hoop` on your PATH + shell completion.
#    Skip this and prefix every command with ./plugins/hoop/cli/hoop.sh instead.
./plugins/hoop/cli/hoop.sh install      # then open a new shell so `hoop` resolves

# 3. Bring up the whole stack. The FIRST run builds both images (a few minutes);
#    later starts are fast. Serves the dashboard at http://localhost:7842/.
hoop start

# 4. (optional) Configure your stack interactively — memory, code-graph RAG,
#    platform MCPs, docs RAG, semantic search, observability, second-brain, etc.
#    This is the /hoop:setup wizard, native to the CLI (needs no host Claude Code).
hoop setup

# 5. One-time: authenticate the sandbox with its OWN Claude account.
#    Drops you into `claude` inside the sandbox — type /login, open the printed
#    URL in your browser, approve, paste the code back, then /exit.
hoop login

然后打开http://localhost:7842/。由于仪表板仅信任本地主机，因此您的浏览器会自动识别为主机 - 访问令牌是为您创建的，无需粘贴。 （远程访问被故意限制；请参阅架构。）

这就是完整的循环：hoop 启动→hoop 设置（可选）→hoop 登录→打开仪表板。 独立路径的一些注意事项：

  • 使用箍设置配置您的堆栈。 这是交互式向导（内存、代码图 RAG、自动化、平台 MCP、文档 RAG、语义搜索、可观察性、设计、第二大脑、遥测隔离）——与 /hoop:setup 后面的向导相同，但原生于 CLI，因此不需要主机 Claude 代码。 它在hoop登录之前运行（写入MCP配置不需要身份验证）。 对于稍后的一次性添加，hoop add mcp/plugin/skill（请参阅 CLI 表）会在重建过程中保留到沙箱配置文件中。
  • 仅终端？ hoop open 在当前目录上的一次性沙箱中运行交互式 claude - 无需仪表板，登录后无需浏览器。
  • 更新： git pull 然后hoop重建将新代码提取到两个图像中。
  • 注销/切换帐户：hoop 注销会清除沙箱的 Claude 登录信息，以便其他帐户可以hoop 登录。

向导做什么

hoop setup（或 /hoop:setup，它会向您指出它）会在顶部同意的情况下引导您完成这些步骤，然后安装每个选择 - 可自动运行和秘密获取的 MCP 立即运行（首先打印的每个命令）； browser-login、plugin-marketplace 和 host-CLI 选项将作为指导步骤打印，您可以自行完成：

# Step Pick
1 同意 Y / N
2 检测先前状态 （只读）
3 记忆 claude-mem（自动安装 - 没有选择）
4 代码图 RAG（如果您编码） Serena / claude-context / 代码图-mcp / Cognee / 跳过
5 自动化 n8n-mcp 是/否
6 平台 MCP 多选：Atlassian、Google Workspace、GitHub、incident.io、Slack
7 RAG文档 上下文7 是/否
8 可观察性 Sentry / Datadog（多选）
9 设计 Excalidraw 是/否
10 第二大脑 Obsidian (3 种口味) / Notion (2) / Logseq / NotebookLM
11 登录 Claude Code /login，然后 gh（设备流）+ 任何排队的 MCP OAuth — 全部在沙箱内

每次运行都会附加到沙箱配置文件的 ~/.claude/hoop/sandbox/profile/.claude/hoop/install-log.md （也可以从仪表板查看），因此重新运行是可审核的。 秘密永远不会到达日志 - 它们直接进入 claude mcp add -e 或 0600 ~/.claude/hoop/hoop.env。

仪表板

/hoop:dashboard （或启动 | 停止 | 重新启动 | 重建 | 状态 | 日志）在容器内运行仪表板。 你的主机只需要 Docker Desktop——没有 Node，没有 npm 安装，没有 Next.js 构建污染。 每个动词都有一个可选的服务目标（全部（默认）·沙箱·仪表板）；开始延迟构建（仅当图像丢失时），而重建总是重建。

配对（邀请队友共同推动会话）使用 cloudflared 通过公共隧道公开本地仪表板 - 它被烘焙到仪表板图像中，因此无需在主机上安装任何内容。 仪表板无需配对即可正常运行；仅共享链接才会启动隧道。

五个面板：

  • 会话 — ~/.claude/sessions/ 上的 fs.watch；实时更新。
  • 技能 - 磁盘上的每项技能（用户+插件），可过滤，只需一键“运行”即可在仪表板容器内生成 claude -p '/<name>' 并将标准输出流回面板。
  • 子代理 - 根据代理工具上的 PreToolUse / PostToolUse 事件重建的嵌套树。 单击节点可查看其提示、工具调用和最终输出。
  • 事件——通过服务器发送的事件按时间顺序实时跟踪。 Hooks 通过 Unix 域套接字将每个事件推送到沙箱的 /ingest；仪表板以零轮询跟踪结果流。
  • 搜索 — 以 ⌘K 打开。 BM25 (FTS5) 始终有效；配置嵌入后端时，语义搜索（sqlite-vec、768-dim 嵌入）会激活。 通过 hoop setup / /hoop:setup 进行设置 — 推荐使用 Docker Model Runner（通过 Compose 的模型添加到 compose 堆栈：元素并在 hoop start 上拉动），并使用 Ollama (nomic-embed-text)、OpenAI 或任何自定义 OpenAI 兼容端点作为替代方案。 混合通过倒数等级融合融合 BM25 + 语义。

仪表板设计为单用户且仅限本地主机；访问由每次安装令牌控制（请参阅下面的架构）。

CLI（希望）

plugins/hoop/cli/ 提供了一个基于 oosh 的小型 CLI，它包装了运行时。 它位于插件内部（框架引擎 + 入口点 + 完成 + lib/stack.sh 中的堆栈引擎），因此它随插件一起提供，并且斜杠命令（/hoop:setup、/hoop:dashboard）可以直接调用它。 它不需要外部安装并解析自己的路径。

./plugins/hoop/cli/hoop.sh install     # symlink `hoop` onto PATH + shell completion (bash/zsh)
# or run in place without installing:
./plugins/hoop/cli/hoop.sh <module> <command>

两级：顶级动词作用于整个堆栈；模块范围仅限于单一服务。 它们都驱动一个引擎 (cli/lib/stack.sh) — 主机端预检的单一事实来源（配置文件绑定安装准备、仪表板/对等身份验证令牌、转发嵌入环境、撰写编排）。 Claude 引导旁路 + 插件/挂钩接线在启动时在沙箱内运行 (sandbox/seed-profile.mjs)，因此主机不需要 jq。 启动/重建是故意分开的——启动仅在图像丢失时构建图像（否则很快），而重建总是重建，以便您拾取代码更改。

呼啦登录·注销 使用自己的 Claude 帐户对沙箱进行身份验证（一次性）。 登录会将您带入运行沙箱内的 claude 中以运行 /login （粘贴代码流程 - 在主机浏览器中批准 URL）；沙箱铸造并自我刷新自己的 OAuth 令牌。 注销会清除它，以便您可以使用其他帐户登录。 您的主机凭据永远不会被读取或复制。

医生 （默认） 通过仅 Docker 独立镜头对主机 + 堆栈进行只读运行状况检查：验证 Docker + Compose v2、报告存在哪些可选子命令工具 (jq/curl/awk)、检查沙箱映像/容器、Claude auth 和语义搜索后端（包括）。 DMR Compose 模型：覆盖+可达性）。 仅在真正阻塞的问题上失败；其他一切都是建议性的。 
hoop start                    # bring up the whole stack at http://localhost:7842/
hoop setup                    # interactive wizard: configure the sandbox stack (no host Claude Code needed)
hoop login                    # one-time: authenticate the sandbox with its own Claude account
hoop dashboard rebuild        # rebuild + recreate ONLY the dashboard container
hoop sandbox rebuild          # rebuild + recreate ONLY the agent-sandbox container
hoop open                     # interactive claude in a sandbox over $PWD
hoop add mcp context7 -- npx -y @upstash/context7-mcp   # install an MCP (user scope) into the sandbox
hoop add skill -d ~/.claude/skills/impeccable           # copy a local skill into the sandbox profile
hoop mount add -p ~/code/myproject                      # expose a host folder to the sandbox workspace

add / mount 子命令也公开为 /hoop:add 和 /hoop:mount 斜杠命令，它们委托给此 CLI。 添加的所有内容都位于 ~/.claude/hoop/sandbox/profile 中（绑定安装在 /home/agent 中），因此安装一次的组件在每个仪表板会话和oop打开中都可用，并且在图像重建后仍然存在。

hoop open 使用 hoop-sandbox 镜像（使用 hoop sandbox 重建构建一次）并安装沙箱 Claude 配置文件（~/.claude/hoop/sandbox/profile，-p\|--profile 进行覆盖），因此 claude 已经经过身份验证 - 运行 hoop login 一次以使用自己的 Claude 帐户对沙箱进行身份验证。 与仪表板的代理沙箱不同，开放式运行带有遥测黑洞，并且没有hoop的仪表板挂钩/插件（需要仪表板的套接字），因此这是一个干净、隔离的交互式会话，仍然具有您设置的MCP和技能。 hoop install / hoop uninstall 管理 PATH 符号链接和 shell 接线；存储库本身从未被修改。

浏览器自动化

hoop-sandbox 镜像附带一个无头 Chromium + 官方 @playwright/mcp，自动在沙盒配置文件中注册（从容器内部，通过 claude mcp add，因此注册不能指向正在运行的镜像没有的浏览器）。 代理获取 browser_navigate、browser_click、browser_type、browser_fill_form、browser_snapshot、browser_take_screenshot 等，由完全在容器内以非特权代理用户身份运行的浏览器驱动。 没有主机浏览器，没有主机进程，没有 host.docker.internal 体操，并且用户上下文中没有任何东西运行。 由于两个表面支持相同的图像和配置文件，因此浏览器工具在每个仪表板会话和打开的环中都可用。

两种刻意的强化选择：

  • 短暂的轮廓（--隔离）。 浏览器配置文件保存在内存中，并且永远不会写入磁盘，因此会话之间不会保留任何 cookie 或登录信息，也没有周围的环境身份验证。 要驱动需要登录的站点，请向浏览器工具提供一个 --storage-state 文件（请参阅 @playwright/mcp 文档）。
  • 没有任意代码工具。 @playwright/mcp 将 browser_run_code_unsafe （Playwright 进程中的任意 JavaScript，RCE 等效项）公开为不可移除的“核心”功能。 hoop 通过沙箱 settings.json 中 Claude 自己的 Permissions.deny 拒绝它，因此模型永远不会看到它 - 工具集的其余部分不受影响。

配对和计划审查

/hoop:dashboard 可以通过 cloudflared 隧道将共享链接传递给队友。 他们打开门，选一个名字，主人就会接纳他们；从那时起，双方都会看到相同的实时记录，并且可以通过笔记本电脑或手机聊天（>前缀）或共同驱动模型。

使用 /plan <task> 运行一轮，沙盒强制代理只读：它进行调查，然后提交在审查面板中打开的计划。 主持人和任何全能同行都可以删除锚定到确切段落的内联评论（在每个人之间实时同步），然后批准或请求更改。 拒绝会反馈意见，代理商会修改计划。

主机流程，端到端
1·聊天——一个同伴加入，双方在线程中聊天（>前缀）；不花费模型回合。 2 · 提示——主机向模型发送正常转向。
3 · 澄清——座席在计划之前提出结构化问题。 4 · 观察 — 每个挂钩事件都会流入实时事件面板。
5 · Plan — /plan 强制代理只读；它起草计划而不是采取行动。 6 · 审核 — 该计划将打开以供内联评论，然后批准/请求更改。

7·迭代——“请求变更”反馈意见；代理修改为只读并重新提交，一旦主机批准，会话将离开计划模式并实施。

同事们从任何地方共同主持同一会议 - 这是“Rafael”通过他的手机审查该计划：

建筑学

运行时分为两个容器，因此 Web 层的妥协无法到达您的凭据：

  • agent-sandbox（受信任）- 拥有 claude 二进制文件、您的 Claude 配置文件（OAuth 凭证、插件、MCP 配置、会话/脚本）、长期存在的 claude -p --input-format=stream-json 子进程和 events.db（唯一编写者）。 它通过 Unix 域套接字 (/var/run/hoop/sandbox.sock) 公开一个小型 HTTP API — 无 TCP 端口。 该模型作为非特权代理用户运行，沙箱实际使用的面向 claude 的插件表面（钩子脚本 + .mcp.json 工具服务器 + 插件清单）被烘焙到位于 /opt/hoop 的沙箱映像中，由 root 拥有，以便用户无法篡改钩子脚本。 故意不烘焙仅主机部分：commands/（主机堆栈斜线命令）和agents/（主机诊断）在沙箱内没有意义，而catalog/+templates/只能由主机上运行的hoop设置读取。 该映像是独立的 - 没有主机存储库绑定安装 - 因此它运行在既没有位于固定路径的存储库也没有安装 Claude Code 的主机上。 （设置 HOOP_PLUGIN_DEV=1 将完整主机存储库覆盖回 /opt/hoop 以便在开发中实时编辑插件源。）
  • 仪表板（不受信任的视图）- Next.js 绑定到 127.0.0.1:7842，没有 claude 二进制文件，也无法访问 ~/.claude。 每个 API 路由都是一个瘦代理，通过套接字调用沙箱，因此受感染的仪表板只能执行沙箱 API 允许的操作。

events.db 保留了事实来源：沙箱写入它（钩子在那里触发），仪表板仅通过套接字读取它。 三个令牌控制三个跃点：

Token Hop 标头
仪表板.token 浏览器 ↔ 仪表板 x-仪表板-令牌（+ cookie）
沙盒令牌 仪表板 ↔ 沙箱 x-沙箱-令牌
钩子令牌 钩子脚本 ↔ 沙箱/摄取 x-hook-令牌

这就是“沙盒代理”模型：操作系统进程边界就是安全边界，仪表板不包含任何秘密。 通过 cloudflared 和点对点共享代币进行配对（与队友共同驾驶会话）。

插件写入的状态

~/.claude/hoop/
  hoop.env                    opt-in overrides forwarded into the sandbox (0600):
                              OPENAI_API_KEY / EMBEDDING_BASE_URL / EMBEDDING_MODEL /
                              EMBED_DIM / telemetry switches
  host-gateway.cache          cached host.docker.internal address
  shares.json                 active pairing share links
  sandbox/profile/            the sandbox's Claude HOME (bind-mounted to /home/agent)
    .claude/
      .credentials.json       the sandbox's OWN Claude OAuth token (never the host's)
      hoop/
        events.db             SQLite (FTS5 + sqlite-vec); the dashboard reads it over the socket
        events.jsonl          append-only event audit log + replay buffer if the dashboard is down
        install-log.md        audit trail of every `hoop setup` run
        profile.md            identity + installed-stack summary written by the wizard
~/.local/share/hoop/          per-install secrets: dashboard.token, peer-signing.secret (0600)

因为沙箱的 HOME 是从 sandbox/profile/ 绑定安装的，所以容器写入自己的 ~/.claude/hoop/ 的所有内容都位于主机上的嵌套路径下 - 代理拥有所有运行时状态；仪表板只能通过套接字读取它。

该插件不会编辑您的 ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md 或 ~/.claude/settings.json。

挂钩管道

沙箱在启动时播种自己的配置文件（sandbox/seed-profile.mjs，由容器入口点使用图像的烘焙节点运行 - 无主机 jq）：它在 PreToolUse、PostToolUse、SessionStart、Stop 和 UserPromptSubmit 上连接 settings.json 挂钩 - 在沙箱配置文件中声明（而不是在主机 hooks.json 中），因此它们仅在沙箱内运行，而绝不会在您的主机上运行。 每个事件都运行 hooks/scripts/emit-event.sh 其中：

  1. 通过curl --max-time 1 通过 Unix 域套接字 (--unix-socket) 将 JSON 事件发布到沙箱的 /ingest 端点 - 推送，而不是轮询。 （HOOP_INGEST_URL 可以覆盖旧版/开发设置的目标。）
  2. 如果套接字无法访问，则退回到附加到 events.jsonl。
  3. <50ms 内退出 — 纯 bash + curl，无 node/python/jq。

沙箱是唯一的编写者：它的摄取路由持续到 SQLite + FTS5（如果启用了语义搜索，则为 sqlite-vec），然后在进程内 EventEmitter 上发出，该 EventEmitter 将仪表板代理的 SSE 流提供给浏览器。 启动时，摄取器会从其保存的偏移量中耗尽 events.jsonl ，以便在仪表板关闭时自动重放写入的事件。

hooks/scripts/permission-gate.sh (PreToolUse) 是唯一的工具权限门：它通过相同的套接字向沙箱询问并阻止，直到主机（或允许决定的对等点）响应 - 这就是支持 /plan 的只读执行和下面的计划审查批准流程的原因。

路线图

  • v0.1（此版本）：向导、容器化仪表板、基于推送的事件管道、BM25 + 选择加入语义搜索。
  • v0.2：将技能触发器注入现有的克劳德会话中，而不是产生新的会话；通过临时容器按技能运行进行隔离。
  • v0.3+：更多目录条目； MCP 重叠的非 Claude 客户端（Cursor、Codex）。
